frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis (MVPA)
frameworks.one_line: decoding memorized shape identity from distributed fMRI activity patterns

frameworks.name: adaptive prefrontal working memory code
frameworks.one_line: dorsolateral prefrontal cortex stores contents in flexible abstract format

frameworks.name: sensory recruitment of posterior cortex
frameworks.one_line: posterior areas maintain contents in perception-like representations

frameworks.name: view-invariant object representation
frameworks.one_line: rotation-invariant code tested as index of representational abstraction

core_question: Do prefrontal and posterior working-memory representations of complex shapes differ in their level of view-invariant abstraction?
key_assumptions: rotation-invariance indexes abstraction in neural representations
fMRI multivariate patterns reflect memorized content
dlPFC and frontal eye fields are dissociable frontal substrates
manipulations: shape identity held in working memory
anatomical region of interest (posterior vs frontal subregions)
key_findings: both posterior and frontal regions support decoding of memorized shape identity
memory representations are rotation-invariant in both regions
frontal contribution localizes to frontal eye fields rather than dlPFC
interpretations: view-independent working memory code is shared across posterior and frontal cortex
frontal eye fields, not dlPFC, host the abstract frontal representation
